findingDetails.title: 010 canonical docs still describe the pre-fix stub state
findingDetails.evidence: Core packet docs and metadata still point at the original deep-research stub and old timestamp; rg found no FIX-010-v1 or Planning Packet metadata in canonical docs.
findingDetails.recommendation: Update canonical 010 docs/metadata to record FIX-010-v1 review state, current lineage, and next safe action, or explicitly mark review artifacts as the current source of truth.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Checked spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md, implementation-summary.md, description.json, and graph-metadata.json; rg for FIX-010-v1/session/Planning Packet terms returned no matches across those core docs.

findingDetails.title: Active review lineage is missing deep-review-strategy.md
findingDetails.evidence: The deep-review state contract requires deep-review-strategy.md under the review artifact directory, but the active 010 review packet lacks that file while config/state JSONL exist.
findingDetails.recommendation: Restore or regenerate review/deep-review-strategy.md for the active lineage so next focus and exhausted-surface traceability are externalized.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Checked active review artifact path with test -e for deep-review-strategy.md and read existing config/state files; strategy file is absent.

findingDetails.title: Finding-class enum is documented upstream but not centralized or enforced at the deep-review/plan boundary
findingDetails.evidence: sk-code-review and the R5 checklist enumerate findingClass values, but deep-review state guidance only requires the field, review-mode contract only requires findingDetails, post-dispatch validation only checks findingDetails is an array, and /spec_kit:plan imports the field without restating or validating the enum.
findingDetails.recommendation: Centralize the findingClass enum or validate the shared values anywhere findingDetails enters reducer/report synthesis so future producer drift is detected before /spec_kit:plan consumes it.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Same-class producer inventory found enum definitions in sk-code-review and the checklist, field propagation in @deep-review, state docs, R7 synthesis, and R3 plan prompts, and no scopeProofNeeded occurrences on active surfaces; validation reads only the array shape.

findingDetails.title: Planning Packet fields are imported into planner context without a data-only boundary
findingDetails.evidence: R7 builds activeFindings, findingClasses, and affectedSurfacesSeed from reducer-owned state plus JSONL findingDetails; R3 maps activeFindings[].findingClass, activeFindings[].scopeProof, and activeFindings[].affectedSurfaceHints into the FIX ADDENDUM before drafting phases, but the planner prompt does not require quoting, escaping, truncating, or treating those values as inert data.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a data-only boundary for Planning Packet imports: quote or serialize imported values, ignore instruction-looking content, and require affected surfaces to be derived from verified paths/symbols rather than executable prose.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Same-class producer and consumer inventory covered sk-code-review, @deep-review, deep-review state docs, both R7 synthesis workflows, both R3 plan workflows, and the plan template; the flow is wired, but hostile-content handling is not specified.

findingDetails.title: Review prompt artifacts retain absolute local workspace paths
findingDetails.evidence: The R3/R7 prompt templates inspected use relative repository paths, but active 010 review prompt artifacts store absolute /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/... write targets for iterations 001-004.
findingDetails.recommendation: Emit relative packet paths in persisted review prompt artifacts or redact the workspace prefix before writing prompts intended for repository storage.
findingDetails.scopeProof: rg for absolute workspace markers found no matches in the R3/R7 command templates or deep-review agent/state surfaces inspected, but found the same absolute output paths in 010 review prompt artifacts for iterations 001-004.

results.evidence: Classified security review as cross-consumer plus matrix/evidence and ran same-class producer, consumer, and prompt-artifact path inventories.
findingClass, scopeProof, and affectedSurfaceHints flow from review findingDetails into R7 Planning Packet synthesis and both R3 plan modes' FIX ADDENDUM import/render guidance.
No direct shell/eval execution of affectedSurfacesSeed, activeFindings, findingClass, scopeProof, or affectedSurfaceHints was found in the R3 plan workflow surfaces inspected.
The same fields are imported into planner context without explicit data-only handling, quoting, escaping, truncation, or hostile-content rejection guidance.
No absolute workspace paths were found in the R3/R7 command templates inspected; active 010 review prompt artifacts retain absolute /Users/... output paths.

noveltyJustification: Security re-verification found the field handoff closed but identified one prompt-injection boundary gap and one persisted prompt path disclosure advisory.
ruledOut: missing R7 Planning Packet field synthesis guidance
missing R3 FIX ADDENDUM import guidance
direct shell execution of affected surface fields in R3 plan workflow
absolute workspace path leak in R3/R7 command templates inspected
